title: Direct imaging and electronic structure modulation of moiré superlattices at the 2D/3D interface
type1_summary: No clear Type 1 cleaned, replot-ready scientific data files were identified in the inspected subset. The archive contents are dominated by software/app metadata and resources.
type2_summary: No clear Type 2 raw scientific measurement files were identified in the inspected subset. The only scientific-looking items are supplementary PDFs, which are documentation rather than raw instrument data.
classification_notes: The inspected files appear to be a 4DSTEM Explorer application bundle plus supplementary PDFs, not the actual raw or cleaned dataset tables/images from the paper. The paper itself clearly contains raw 4D STEM and microscopy data, but those specific files were not present in the inspected subset. Because only the first 20 of 50 files were shown and no machine-readable scientific data files were visible, classification is conservative.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies epitaxially grown MoS2/Au{111} and hBN/Au{111} 2D/3D interfaces, showing that conventional TEM can make the moiré appear to have an 18 Å period while the true crystallographic moiré is 32 Å once the 3D stacking of Au is accounted for. By combining HRTEM, iDPC STEM, 4D STEM, geometric convolution analysis, and DFT, the authors directly image the moiré superlattice and show that the interfacial charge density modulation follows the 32 Å periodicity.
measurement_types: Fast Fourier transform (FFT) of TEM images
Density functional theory (DFT) electronic structure calculation
Band unfolding / density of states calculation

figures.description: Schematic of epitaxially aligned Au nanoislands grown on suspended MoS2 on a SiNx TEM grid.
Reciprocal-space model and experimental SAED pattern showing Au{111} aligned with MoS2{0001}, including weak 1/3{422} Au spots.
HRTEM image of an Au nanoisland on MoS2 showing an apparent 18 Å moiré pattern.
HAADF STEM image of the same interface, showing moiré contrast and coalescence boundaries.
Atomic model of the 32 Å moiré viewed along [100], highlighting inequivalent Au stacking sites.
Plan-view atomic model of the 32 Å moiré with three inequivalent sites boxed.
Close-up plan-view atomic models of the three inequivalent moiré sites.
Radial distribution functions for the three inequivalent 32 Å moiré sites.
HAADF STEM image showing the apparent 18 Å and true 32 Å moiré cells.
iDPC STEM image showing contrast differences that resolve the 32 Å moiré periodicity.
Relative intensity distributions/statistics for inequivalent sites in the HAADF image.
Relative intensity distributions/statistics for inequivalent sites in the iDPC image.
Schematic of the geometric convolution construction for generating moiré frequencies from two lattices.
Calculated moiré period versus rotation angle for the four largest MoS2/Au{111} moiré supercells, including strain bounds.
FFT of the HRTEM image showing moiré satellite peaks around the central beam.
Simulated FFT/diffraction pattern from the geometric convolution model, matching the experimental FFT.
Schematic of 4D STEM acquisition with rastered probe and diffraction pattern recorded at each position.
Averaged CBED pattern from the 4D STEM scan area.
Virtual ADF annulus selecting diffraction spots used to reconstruct the 18 Å moiré image.
Virtual ADF annulus selecting weaker diffraction spots used to reconstruct the 32 Å moiré image.
Virtual ADF STEM image revealing the 18 Å moiré periodicity.
Virtual ADF STEM image revealing the 32 Å moiré periodicity.
DFT charge-density-difference isosurface for the MoS2/Au{111} commensurate moiré viewed in cross section.
Calculated charge-density difference map showing modulation with the 32 Å moiré periodicity.
Unfolded band structure and density of states for MoS2/Au{111}.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include SAED, HRTEM images, HAADF STEM images, iDPC STEM images, and 4D STEM datasets (a CBED pattern at each probe position). The paper also mentions AFM measurements of island thickness. Experimental imaging was performed on TEM/STEM instruments at specified voltages and detector settings.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: Processed outputs include FFTs of HRTEM images, statistical intensity histograms and site partitioning for inequivalent moiré sites, virtual ADF reconstructions from 4D STEM, geometric-convolution-predicted moiré-period curves and simulated diffraction patterns, multislice simulations, and DFT-derived charge-density-difference maps plus unfolded band structures/DOS.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The authors state that the main data supporting the findings are available within the article and its Supplementary Information files. Code is available upon request from the authors.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described as microscopy measurements acquired on TEM/STEM instruments: SAED, HRTEM, HAADF STEM, iDPC STEM, and 4D STEM CBED datasets. The paper gives acquisition conditions and some processing steps, but does not state that raw image stacks or raw 4D datasets are deposited as separate files.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include FFTs of HRTEM images, virtual ADF images reconstructed from 4D STEM, intensity distributions and statistical tests for inequivalent moiré sites, simulated FFTs/diffraction patterns from geometric convolution, multislice simulated STEM images, and DFT-derived charge-density and band-structure results. The paper also mentions that figures and Supplementary Information contain the main supporting data.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are implied to be present in the article and Supplementary Information, but the paper does not explicitly say that per-figure source-data files are deposited. The statement 'main data supporting the findings ... are available within the article and its Supplementary Information files' suggests figure-level supporting data are embedded in the publication package rather than in a separate repository.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: A geometric convolution code was implemented in Wolfram Mathematica 12.0, and custom Python-based STEM image simulation software was used. The paper says code is available upon request, but does not provide a public repository or notebook link.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: Some figure-level results are shown in the paper and Supplementary Information, but the paper does not indicate a public repository with raw microscopy datasets or complete source-data tables. Code is only available upon request, so full replotting from deposited files is not clearly supported.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper clearly distinguishes raw microscopy acquisition from derived analyses: HRTEM images are Fourier transformed to obtain moiré peaks; 4D STEM raw diffraction patterns are post-processed into virtual ADF images; iDPC and HAADF intensities are statistically analyzed after peak detection and watershed segmentation; and DFT outputs are derived from first-principles calculations. The dataset is therefore a mix of experimental raw images/diffraction data and multiple layers of processed/derived outputs, but the public data packaging is limited to article + Supplementary Information, with code only on request.
